decisions.title: Auth module shipped with zero P0 bugs
decisions.detail: The authentication module was delivered last Friday (before 2026-07-02) and had no P0 bugs reported.

evidence.quote: Alex opened by confirming the auth module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.

decisions.title: JWT token expiry set to 24 hours
decisions.detail: The JWT (JSON Web Token) expiry duration is configured for 24 hours. This was previously agreed upon in Sprint 2.

evidence.quote: Ben said the JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours as agreed in Sprint 2.

decisions.title: Password reset emails not rate-limited (risk/missing requirement)
decisions.detail: Password reset emails currently lack rate-limiting, as flagged by Carol. This is a potential vulnerability or unmet requirement.

evidence.quote: Carol flagged that password reset emails are not being rate-limited.

decisions.title: Max 3 password reset attempts per hour
decisions.detail: A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon during the security review held on 2026-06-28.

evidence.quote: Max 3 reset attempts per hour was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28.
Password reset rate limit: 3 per hour

decisions.title: API gateway will use AWS API Gateway with 100 req/min limit per user
decisions.detail: The API gateway will be implemented using AWS API Gateway, enforcing a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user. This was a recommendation from Ben based on load test results.

evidence.quote: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway with a 100 req/min limit per user. This was Ben's recommendation based on load tests.
API gateway: AWS API Gateway, 100 req/min per user cap

decisions.title: Mobile app team blocked by auth API spec publication
decisions.detail: The mobile application development team is currently blocked and cannot proceed until the authentication API specification has been published.

evidence.quote: Alex noted the mobile app team is blocked until the auth API spec is published.

decisions.title: Target publish date for auth API spec is 2026-07-05
decisions.detail: The target date for publishing the authentication API specification is 2026-07-05.

decisions.title: Automated integration tests for all three auth endpoints required before Sprint 3 sign-off
decisions.detail: Carol requires that all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, and reset) have automated integration tests implemented and passed before the Sprint 3 sign-off.

evidence.quote: Carol wants all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) to have automated integration tests before Sprint 3 sign-off.

decisions.title: Fallback behaviour if API gateway fails
decisions.detail: A question about what system behavior or procedures are in place if the API gateway experiences downtime.

evidence.quote: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down?

decisions.title: Refresh token invalidation on password reset
decisions.detail: A question regarding whether existing refresh tokens are invalidated automatically when a user performs a password reset.

evidence.quote: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

implementation_scope: Implement rate-limiting for password reset attempts.
Develop and pass automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints.

constraints.text: Password reset attempts must be limited to a maximum of 3 per hour.
API gateway must enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
Authentication API specification must be published by 2026-07-05 (to unblock mobile app team).
Automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints are required before Sprint 3 sign-off.

architectural_decisions.text: JWT token expiry is configured for 24 hours.
API gateway will be implemented using AWS API Gateway.

open_technical_questions: What is the fallback behavior or system procedures if the AWS API Gateway experiences downtime?
Should existing refresh tokens be invalidated automatically when a user performs a password reset?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user successfully authenticates, When a JWT token is issued, Then the token's expiry timestamp must be exactly 24 hours from its issuance.
Given an authenticated user, When their JWT token's expiry time has passed, Then any subsequent API requests using that token must be rejected with an appropriate unauthorized/expired token error.

Given a user initiates a password reset, When the user has made 3 password reset requests within a 60-minute window, Then any subsequent password reset request within that same 60-minute window for that user must be rejected or queued without sending an email.

Given a user has exceeded the password reset rate limit, When more than 60 minutes have elapsed since their first password reset request in the last window, Then the user can successfully initiate a new password reset request.

Given an authenticated user, When the user makes 100 API requests within a 60-second window, Then the 101st API request within that same 60-second window must be rejected with a rate limit error (HTTP 429 Too Many Requests).
Given a user has been rate-limited by the API gateway, When more than 60 seconds have elapsed since their first request in the last window, Then the user can successfully make another API request.
The automated integration test suite must contain tests covering the login endpoint functionality and security, and these tests must pass.
The automated integration test suite must contain tests covering the refresh endpoint functionality and security, and these tests must pass.
The automated integration test suite must contain tests covering the reset endpoint functionality and security, and these tests must pass.
test_scenarios: Perform a successful user login and verify that the issued JWT token has an expiry time set to exactly 24 hours from issuance.
Attempt to use a JWT token that has expired to access a protected API resource and confirm the request is rejected with an appropriate error.
Initiate 3 successful password reset requests for the same user within a 5-minute period, confirming each request is processed.
Attempt a 4th password reset request for the same user within the same 5-minute period and confirm it is rejected or blocked.
Wait 61 minutes after the first password reset request (from a sequence of 3 within an hour) and then initiate a new password reset for the same user, confirming it is successful.
Make 100 API calls for a single user within 50 seconds, confirming each call is successful.
Make the 101st API call for the same user within the same 50-second window and confirm it fails with a rate limit (e.g., HTTP 429) error.
After being rate-limited, wait 61 seconds from the first request of the rate-limited sequence and then make another API call, confirming it is successful.
Execute the automated integration test suite and verify that all tests specifically for the 'login' endpoint pass.
Execute the automated integration test suite and verify that all tests specifically for the 'refresh' endpoint pass.
Execute the automated integration test suite and verify that all tests specifically for the 'reset' endpoint pass.
edge_cases: Verify JWT token expiry when the system clock is adjusted (e.g., forward or backward).
Initiate concurrent password reset attempts for a single user from multiple sources within the rate-limit window.
Test password reset attempts where some attempts fail due to incorrect email/username (if these count towards the limit).
Test API gateway rate limiting with a burst of requests exactly at the 100 requests per minute boundary.
Verify the specific HTTP status code and error message returned when an API request is rate-limited by the gateway.
Clarify if the API Gateway rate limit operates on a fixed minute window (e.g., reset at :00 seconds) or a rolling window from the first request.
unresolved_behaviors: What is the expected system behavior or fallback procedure if the AWS API Gateway experiences downtime or becomes unavailable?
Are existing refresh tokens automatically invalidated when a user successfully performs a password reset?

decision_history: The authentication module was delivered last Friday (before 2026-07-02) with zero P0 bugs reported, confirming its successful initial release.
The JWT (JSON Web Token) expiry duration is confirmed at 24 hours, as previously agreed in Sprint 2.
A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was decided during the security review held on 2026-06-28 to enhance security.
The API gateway will be implemented using AWS API Gateway, enforcing a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user, based on load test recommendations.
It was decided that automated integration tests for all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, and reset) are a mandatory requirement before Sprint 3 sign-off.
dependencies: The mobile application development team is blocked and cannot proceed until the authentication API specification has been published.
risks: Password reset emails currently lack rate-limiting, which is a confirmed potential vulnerability or unmet security requirement. (Likelihood: Confirmed, Impact: High)
open_questions: What is the defined fallback behavior or procedure if the API gateway experiences downtime? (Owned by PM/Product for business continuity/resilience requirements)
Are existing refresh tokens automatically invalidated when a user performs a password reset? (Owned by PM/QA for security acceptance criteria clarity)
milestones: Automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset auth endpoints must be implemented and passed before Sprint 3 sign-off

system_requirements: The system SHALL configure JWT tokens to expire after 24 hours.
The system SHALL limit password reset attempts to a maximum of 3 attempts per hour per user.
The system, via the API Gateway, SHALL enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
Automated integration tests MUST be implemented and successfully passed for the login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints prior to Sprint 3 sign-off.
data_flows: The Authentication API specification SHALL be published to the Mobile Application development team.
interface_contracts: Authentication service SHALL issue JWTs with an expiry duration of 24 hours (D002).
The authentication API specification covering login, refresh, and reset endpoints SHALL be formally defined and published (inferred - needs confirmation for explicit definition of these endpoints).

constraints.text: The authentication API specification MUST be published by 2026-07-05.

architectural_decisions.text: The API gateway SHALL be implemented using AWS API Gateway.

open_analysis_questions: What is the defined fallback behavior or procedure if the API Gateway experiences downtime?
SHALL existing refresh tokens be automatically invalidated when a user performs a password reset?

stakeholder.business_summary: The core login system has been delivered on time and without critical defects, marking a significant milestone. However, the mobile application team is currently blocked and cannot proceed until the technical details for integrating with this system are published. We also need to address a critical security vulnerability regarding password reset emails.
key_impact: Improved system security and stability through rate limits on user requests and password resets.
Potential delays for the mobile application launch due to outstanding technical documentation, which we aim to publish by July 5th.
Enhanced security for user accounts with a 24-hour session expiry, requiring users to log in again after this period.
An unaddressed security risk exists with password reset emails, potentially leaving our users vulnerable if not resolved promptly.
major_unresolved_questions: What is our plan if the main system handling all incoming requests (API gateway) experiences an outage?
Do existing user sessions (represented by 'refresh tokens') automatically become invalid when a user resets their password, or could old sessions still be active?

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are consistent with the extracted decisions. No hallucinations, critical quality issues, or sensitive content detected. The SA view correctly identifies an inferred item as 'needs confirmation'. The PM view adds inferred ownership to open questions which is appropriate for that persona's actionability.
